# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.

Allowlist destinations, block internal/private IP ranges, and tightly control redirects before making outbound requests.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Validator;
use GuzzleHttp\Client;
use GuzzleHttp\Exception\RequestException;

class PreviewController extends Controller
{
    protected $allowedHosts = [
        'example.com',
        'another-example.com',
    ];

    public function preview(Request $request)
    {
        // Validate the URL input
        $validator = Validator::make($request->all(), [
            'url' => 'required|url',
        ]);

        if ($validator->fails()) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Invalid URL'], 400);
        }

        $url = $request->input('url');

        // Check if the host is allowed
        if (!in_array(parse_url($url, PHP_URL_HOST), $this->allowedHosts)) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'URL not allowed'], 403);
        }

        // Initialize Guzzle HTTP client
        $client = new Client([
            'timeout'  => 10.0,
            'allow_redirects' => false,
        ]);

        try {
            // Send a request to the URL
            $response = $client->request('GET', $url);

            // Check if the response is a redirect
            if ($response->getStatusCode() == 302) {
                return response()->json(['error' => 'Redirects are not allowed'], 403);
            }

            // Get the page content
            $content = (string)$response->getBody();

            // Return the preview
            return response()->json([
                'url' => $url,
                'preview' => $content,
            ]);
        } catch (RequestException $e) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to fetch URL'], 500);
        }
    }
}
